solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests check semantic properties: whether the Uv arm contains a format! call with VERSIONS_MANIFEST_MIRROR before VERSIONS_MANIFEST_URL, and whether ManifestParse/ManifestUtf8 map to true in should_try_next_url. These are behavioral checks that any correct fix would satisfy regardless of exact formatting, variable naming, or line positioning. Alternative implementations (e.g., different brace styles, different ordering of match arms) would still pass.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The tests do NOT compile or execute Rust code. They parse source text via regex to check for patterns like 'format!...VERSIONS_MANIFEST_MIRROR' and whether variant names appear near '=> true'. This is sophisticated text inspection (with comment stripping and brace matching), but it is still source text analysis, not behavioral execution. No Rust compilation or runtime testing occurs.

test_not_tautological.reason: The base commit (before fix) has the Uv arm using only VERSIONS_MANIFEST_URL without VERSIONS_MANIFEST_MIRROR, and should_try_next_url does not include ManifestParse/ManifestUtf8. A stub or no-op would fail these tests since the original source lacks the required patterns. The tests are fail-to-pass: they fail on the original code and pass only after making the specific changes.
